trivia_bullets.text: Snapping Voidcraw is the only card in all of Magic: The Gathering with the type line 'Creature — Eldrazi Turtle.' A Scryfall search for the combined subtype returns exactly one result: this card. [Scryfall: type search t:eldrazi t:turtle, total_cards: 1]

Flavor text: "Once considered a delicacy, turtle soup is no longer served in Nephalia's inns." [Scryfall] — Nephalia is the coastal province of Innistrad, known for port towns, sea commerce, and fog-cloaked harbors. During the Shadows Over Innistrad arc (2016), Emrakul's arrival drove 'horrible monsters from the churning ocean waters to devour fishing vessels' — warping the local fauna. The flavor text is a deadpan in-world footnote: the delicacy is off the menu because the turtles are now tentacled Eldrazi horrors. [Scryfall: MH3-204 flavor_text; Wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Innistrad: Nephalia; WebSearch snippet]
sources: Wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Innistrad: Nephalia (2011)

trivia_bullets.text: Despite costing {1}{G}{U}, Snapping Voidcraw is colorless due to Devoid — and taps to add {C}{C}, producing two colorless mana, making it a mana-positive creature on tap. This design echoes Eye of Ugin and Eldrazi Temple as colorless-mana enablers but in creature form. Pauper-legal (confirmed Scryfall legalities), placing it in the budget-accessible colorless/Eldrazi ramp archetype. EDHREC rank 4057 is notably high for a common, indicating meaningful Commander uptake in colorless and Eldrazi-tribal decks. [Scryfall: MH3-204 legalities, oracle_text; EDHREC rank]

Artist Camille Alquier is a French illustrator and Wizards of the Coast contractor who has illustrated multiple MTG cards. No Hipsters of the Coast artist-spotlight piece on Alquier was found in this pass. [Scryfall: MH3-204 artist field; Camille Alquier ArtStation profile]
